title: छठ पूजा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री छठ पूजा स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ छठी मैया स्तुति ॥

केलहुँ छठि मैया शरण तिहारी।
करुणा कय दृष्टि सदा सुखकारी॥१॥

छठि मैया के जस गावत साधु।
दिवस रैन सब करत अराधू॥२॥

॥ सूर्य स्तुति (छठ) ॥

उगते सूरज देव के अरघ करीं हम दान।
छठि मैया भईली प्रसन्न, लिहली सब कै मान॥३॥

सूर्यदेव प्रणमाम्यहं सहस्रकिरणप्रभम्।
सप्ताश्ववाहनं देवं छठव्रतफलप्रदम्॥४॥

नमो सूर्याय शान्ताय सर्वरोगनिवारिणे।
छठीव्रतप्रसन्नाय आयुष्यं देहि भास्करम्॥५॥

षष्ठीदेवी महाभागा सूर्यपत्नी यशस्विनी।
सन्तानसौख्यदात्री च षष्ठीदेव्यै नमोऽस्तु ते॥६॥

कार्तिकशुक्लषष्ठ्यां तु सूर्यमाराधयेद् बुधः।
सन्ध्यायामर्घ्यदानेन सर्वपापक्षयो भवेत्॥७॥

नद्यां स्नात्वा शुचिर्भूत्वा सूर्यं सम्पूज्य विधिवत्।
ठेकुआ प्रसादं निवेद्य छठी माता प्रसीदतु॥८॥

ॐ ह्रां ह्रीं ह्रौं सः सूर्याय नमः।
छठी मैया की जय। सूर्यदेव की जय॥

अस्तगामी सूर्यदेव अर्घ्यं गृहाण।
ऊगत सूरज अर्घ्य कय, बोलें जय हो मातु।
छठ मैया व्रत करके, सूख पाबें बाटु॥९॥

सूर्यमण्डलमध्यस्थं षष्ठीदेवीं नमाम्यहम्।
व्रतेन सन्तुष्टहृदा पुत्रपौत्रप्रदायिनीम्॥१०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
छठव्रतमिदं पुण्यं सूर्यषष्ठीसमन्वितम्।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय आरोग्यं सन्ततिं लभेत्॥

॥ इति श्री छठ पूजा स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Chhaṭh Pūjā Stotram ||

|| Chhaṭhī Maiyā Stuti ||

Kelahūṁ Chhaṭhi Maiyā śaraṇa tihārī.
Karuṇā kay dṛṣṭi sadā sukhakārī.||1||

Chhaṭhi Maiyā ke jas gāvat sādhu.
Divas rain sab karat arādhū.||2||

|| Sūrya Stuti (Chhaṭh) ||

Ugate Sūraj Dev ke aragha karīṁ ham dān.
Chhaṭhi Maiyā bhaīlī prasanna, lihalī sab kai mān.||3||

Sūryadeva praṇāmyahaṁ sahasrakiraṇaprabham.
Saptāśvavāhanaṁ devaṁ Chhaṭhavrataphalapradam.||4||

Namo Sūryāya śāntāya sarvaroganivāriṇe.
Chhaṭhīvrataprasannāya āyuṣyaṁ dehi Bhāskaram.||5||

Ṣaṣṭhīdevī mahābhāgā Sūryapatnī yaśasvinī.
Santānasaukhyadātrī cha Ṣaṣṭhīdevyai namo'stu te.||6||

Kārtikaśuklaṣaṣṭhyāṁ tu Sūryamārādhaayed budhaḥ.
Sandhyāyāmarghyadānena sarvapāpakṣayo bhavet.||7||

Nadyāṁ snātvā śuchirbhūtvā Sūryaṁ sampūjya vidhivat.
Ṭhekuā prasādaṁ nivedya Chhaṭhī Mātā prasīdatu.||8||

Om Hrāṁ Hrīṁ Hrauṁ Saḥ Sūryāya Namaḥ.
Chhaṭhī Maiyā kī Jai. Sūryadeva kī Jai.

Astagāmī Sūryadeva arghyaṁ gṛhāṇa.
Ūgat Sūraj arghya kay, bolen Jai ho Mātu.
Chhaṭh Maiyā vrat karke, sūkh pāben bāṭu.||9||

Sūryamaṇḍalamadhyasthaṁ ṣaṣṭhīdevīṁ namāmyaham.
Vratena santuṣṭahṛdā putrapautrapradāyinīm.||10||

|| Phalaśruti ||
Chhaṭhavratamidaṁ puṇyaṁ Sūryaṣaṣṭhīsamanvitam.
Yaḥ paṭhet prātarutthāya ārogyaṁ santatiṁ labhet.

|| Iti Shrī Chhaṭh Pūjā Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: (1-2) छठी मैया की शरण में आया हूँ, आपकी करुणा दृष्टि सदा सुखकारी है। साधुजन दिन-रात छठी मैया का यश गाते और आराधना करते हैं। (3) उगते सूरज को अर्घ्य दान करते हैं, छठी मैया प्रसन्न होकर सबकी मान लेती हैं। (4) सहस्र किरणों की प्रभा वाले, सप्ताश्व वाहन वाले, छठ व्रत का फल देने वाले सूर्यदेव को प्रणाम। (5) शान्त, सभी रोगों को दूर करने वाले, छठी व्रत से प्रसन्न होने वाले भास्कर से आयुष्य की प्रार्थना। (6) षष्ठीदेवी महाभागा, सूर्यपत्नी, यशस्विनी, सन्तान-सुख देने वाली षष्ठीदेवी को नमस्कार। (7) कार्तिक शुक्ल षष्ठी को सूर्य की आराधना करने वाले बुद्धिमान को सन्ध्या में अर्घ्यदान से सर्वपाप नष्ट होते हैं। (8) नदी में स्नान कर शुद्ध होकर, सूर्य की विधिवत पूजा कर, ठेकुआ प्रसाद अर्पित करने से छठी माता प्रसन्न होती हैं। (10) सूर्यमण्डल में विराजमान, व्रत से सन्तुष्ट, पुत्र-पौत्र प्रदान करने वाली षष्ठीदेवी को नमस्कार।
benefits: Invokes health, longevity, and progeny through chhath vrat
Combines worship of Surya Dev and Chhathi Maiya (Shashti Devi)
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins through arghya offering at sunrise and sunset
Specifically blesses with children and family happiness
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and grants long life
Traditional Chhath Puja prayers in both Bhojpuri and Sanskrit
description: Dedicated to Sun God Surya, the Chhath Puja Stotram (छठ पूजा स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Chhath Puja Tradition / Bhojpuri-Sanskrit Folk Compilation. Regular recitation is believed to destroy all sins through arghya offering at sunrise and sunset, combine worship of Surya Dev and Chhathi Maiya (Shashti Devi), and specifically blesses with children and family happiness. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Chhath Puja for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Chhath Puja Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Chhath Puja Stotram and what does it celebrate?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chhath Puja Stotram (छठ पूजा स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Surya (the Sun God) and Chhathi Maiya (revered as Surya's consort or sister, identified with Devi Shashthi — the Mother-protector of children). It is recited during the Chhath Mahaparva — the four-day vrat observed on Kartik Shukla Chaturthi to Saptami (October-November), considered the most rigorous Hindu vrat, especially in Bihar, Jharkhand, eastern Uttar Pradesh and the Madhesh region of Nepal, and now performed by the Bihari and Purvanchali diaspora across India and worldwide. The stotram invokes Surya as the visible Pratyaksha-Devata (visible god) — the source of all life, light, health and time — and prays for the welfare and long life of the devotee's family, especially children and the husband. It is sung at the Chhath ghat (riverbank, pond or water-tank) facing the rising and setting sun while standing in the water. (Source: Chhath Puja tradition; Bhojpuri-Sanskrit folk-stotra compilation.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the four days of Chhath Puja and which day is each ritual performed?
seoFaqs.answer: Chhath Puja is observed over four sacred days: (1) Day 1 — Nahay Khay (Kartik Shukla Chaturthi): the vratis (those observing the vrat) bathe in the river or sacred water, eat one pure sattvic meal of rice-dal-pumpkin (kaddu-bhat) cooked themselves on a clay stove with mango wood, and prepare for the fast; (2) Day 2 — Lohanda or Kharna (Kartik Shukla Panchami): a full-day fast without water, broken at evening with a special prasad of jaggery-rice kheer (gur-cheelni) cooked in earthen pots, after which the 36-hour nirjala (waterless) fast begins; (3) Day 3 — Sandhya Arghya (Kartik Shukla Shashthi, the central day): vratis stand in waist-deep water at the ghat at sunset and offer arghya (water libation) with sugarcane, banana, coconut, thekua (jaggery-flour cookie), kasar laddu and other prasad to the setting Sun while singing Chhath geet; (4) Day 4 — Usha Arghya (Kartik Shukla Saptami): vratis return to the ghat before dawn and offer arghya to the rising Sun, breaking the long fast after the offering. (Source: Drikpanchang Chhath calendar; Bihar-UP traditional vrat paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Chhathi Maiya and what is her relationship with Surya?
seoFaqs.answer: Chhathi Maiya (Chhath Mata) is the principal goddess invoked alongside Surya during Chhath Puja. She is identified in folk and Puranic traditions in three converging ways: (1) as Devi Shashthi — the goddess Shashthi (sixth-day mother) mentioned in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana (Prakriti Khanda), who is the protector of children and the granter of progeny, traditionally worshipped on the sixth day after a child's birth (chhathi sanskar); (2) as the elder sister or sometimes consort of Surya — in folk tradition Chhathi Maiya is Surya's beloved bahin (sister) who blesses devotees on his behalf; (3) as the Manas-putri (mind-born daughter) of Brahma, born to nourish all life. Her role in Chhath Puja is to grant the welfare of children, fertility to childless women, recovery from illness, and family prosperity — while Surya is invoked for cosmic blessings of light, life and longevity. The Chhath ghat is sanctified by the chanting of Hey Chhathi Maiya throughout the four days. (Source: Brahma Vaivarta Purana Prakriti Khanda; Bihar folk tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Chhath Puja celebrated and is it observed elsewhere besides Kartik?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal Chhath Puja is observed on Kartik Shukla Shashthi (the sixth day of the bright fortnight of Kartik month, October-November) — six days after Diwali Amavasya. This is the Kartik Chhath, considered the supreme Chhath Mahaparva, observed by tens of millions of Bihari, Jharkhandi and Purvanchali Hindus. There is also a second Chhath observed in spring on Chaitra Shukla Shashthi (March-April), called Chaiti Chhath, observed by the same communities though on a smaller scale. Beyond these two principal Chhaths, the deity Chhathi Maiya is worshipped on the sixth day after childbirth (chhathi sanskar) in many North Indian Hindu families. The Kartik Chhath now enjoys the status of an official state holiday in Bihar, Jharkhand and parts of Uttar Pradesh, with millions converging at the Ganga ghats of Patna, Banaras, Munger and the ghats of Delhi, Mumbai and other diaspora cities. (Source: Drikpanchang Chhath calendar; traditional vrat panchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the special prasad offered during Chhath Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: Chhath Puja prasad is famous for its purity, simplicity and exact ritual specifications. The offering thali (basket — sup) at the ghat traditionally contains: (1) Thekua (also called khajuria) — a deep-fried dry sweet made of whole-wheat flour, jaggery, ghee and ghee-fried fennel, considered the supreme Chhath bhog, prepared by the vrati herself in a sacred kitchen using mango-wood fire; (2) Kasar laddu — a roasted-rice-flour and jaggery sweet; (3) Sugarcane — whole stalks (the symbol of sweetness and Surya's autumn harvest); (4) Banana — whole bunches (symbol of fruitfulness); (5) Coconut — whole, blessed and offered to Surya; (6) Seasonal fruits — apple, orange, custard apple, pomegranate, water chestnut (singhada), kaddu (pumpkin-shape boltta), suthni (sweet potato); (7) Jaggery (gur), turmeric root, ginger, betel-leaf, betel-nut. All preparation must be ritually pure: vratis cook in clay vessels on mango wood, no salt or onion-garlic is used, and no one tastes the prasad before offering. (Source: traditional Chhath paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Chhath Puja considered the most rigorous Hindu vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: Chhath Puja is considered the most rigorous Hindu vrat (vrat) for several reasons. (1) Duration: the central fast spans approximately 36 hours, from the morning of Day 2 (after the kharna meal) through Day 4 dawn (after the Usha arghya), entirely nirjala — without food or even a drop of water. (2) Discipline: the vrati must follow strict purity — sleeping on the floor on a mat, no leather, no synthetic clothing, no tasting of prasad, no anger or harsh speech, separation from non-vratis during cooking. (3) Physical austerity: vratis stand in cold river-water at sunset and dawn (in October-November Bihar can be quite cool), waist-deep, often after lying prostrate full-length on the ghat path (dandavat-pranama) during their approach. (4) No mediating priest: the vrati personally cooks all prasad, personally offers arghya, personally bathes — no purohit ritual specialist intervenes, making Chhath one of the few major Hindu vrats with no priestly intermediary. (5) Universality: men, women, young and old — even children — observe it equally seriously. The depth of devotion is legendary. (Source: traditional Bihar-UP-Jharkhand Chhath tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Chhath Puja Stotram and the vrat grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following benefits to observing the Chhath vrat and reciting the Chhath Puja Stotram: (1) santana-prapti and santana-rakshana — children for childless couples and protection of existing children (Chhathi Maiya is the supreme protector of children, blessing them with health, intelligence and long life); (2) suhag-raksha — protection of the husband's life and the marital bond, similar to Karva Chauth; (3) family prosperity, abundance of food, success in agriculture and business; (4) recovery from chronic illness — Chhath is famously recommended for those afflicted with skin diseases, eye ailments, and respiratory troubles (all areas under Surya's domain); (5) Surya-graha-shanti — peace from afflicted Sun in horoscope (vital for confidence, governmental favour, paternal happiness, and authority); (6) sarva-papa-vinashana — destruction of all sins through arghya offering; (7) progress of departed ancestors (pitrigana) since Surya is the lord of the ancestral abode; (8) the supreme inner benefit — direct experience of devotion to the Pratyaksha Devata (the visible Sun) felt through the body during the long austerity. (Source: Chhath Puja phalashruti; Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Bihar folk tradition.)
